features.summary: Amazon reported Q2 2022 net sales of $121.2 billion, up 10% year-over-year excluding FX headwinds, exceeding guidance. Operating income was $3.3 billion, above the top end of guidance range. AWS grew 33% to $19.7 billion with operating income of $5.7 billion. The company made progress reducing incremental costs from $6 billion in Q1 to $4 billion in Q2 compared to prior year, driven by improved fulfillment network productivity and better staffing alignment. Third-party sellers reached 57% of units sold, the highest percentage ever.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q3 operating income of $0-3.5 billion with Prime Day shifting from Q2 to Q3 this year. They anticipate $1.5 billion in sequential fulfillment cost improvements offset by AWS infrastructure investments and increased digital content spending for Thursday Night Football and Lord of the Rings. The company has slowed 2022-2023 operations expansion plans to align with demand and expects fixed cost leverage to improve in the second half as they grow into existing capacity. AWS remains in early stages of enterprise adoption with strong long-term growth opportunity.
features.risks: Inflationary pressures continue at elevated levels including higher fuel, trucking, and shipping costs expected to persist into Q3. New energy cost pressures emerged from rising natural gas prices affecting AWS data centers. Foreign exchange headwinds of 390 basis points are expected in Q3, larger than the 320 basis points impact in Q2. Stock-based compensation increased 150 basis points quarter-over-quarter due to wage inflation for technical employees, and macroeconomic uncertainty could impact future performance despite current strong demand.
features.key_drivers: Prime membership remains strong with improved retention following fee increases, supported by better in-stock levels and delivery speeds. AWS backlog grew 65% year-over-year with weighted average contract life extending to 3.9 years, indicating strong customer commitment. Advertising revenue showed continued strength with high efficiency at point-of-purchase driving growth. International expansion continues in emerging markets like India and Brazil, while established markets show improving profitability over time.
